duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AccessControlHooks.onQueueWithdrawal missing isHooked market validation allows any external caller to trigger credential state manipulation and stateful provider calls
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 13 threats describe the same root cause: AccessControlHooks.onQueueWithdrawal lacks the _hookedMarkets[msg.sender].isHooked check present in onDeposit, onTransfer, and FixedTermLoanHooks.onQueueWithdrawal. Variants focus on different consequences (credential burning, stateful provider calls, reentrancy enablement) but the enabling defect is identical. TECH-H001 is the most comprehensive, referencing both contracts for comparison with specific line references.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Gradual APR step-down bypasses temporary reserve ratio protection by reducing APR ≤25% every 2 weeks, resetting the baseline after each expiry
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same exploit: _calculateTemporaryReserveRatioBips returns originalReserveRatioBips when relativeDiff <= 2500 (25%), and after 2-week expiry the new lower APR becomes the baseline. TECH-H009 describes the expiry mechanism enabling this; ECON-H011 describes clearing expired state first — both are the same underlying design flaw. Note: TECH2-H007 here refers to the entry titled 'Borrower can bypass temporary reserve ratio penalty via step-down APR reductions'. TECH-H002 selected for most comprehensive technical and mathematical analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Shared global _lenderStatus mapping across all markets on the same hooks instance enables cross-market credential leakage
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same design issue: _lenderStatus is mapping(address => LenderStatus) without a market dimension, so a credential granted for market A works for market B if both share the same hooks instance. Note: TECH2-H005 here refers to the entry titled 'Shared _lenderStatus across multiple markets allows unintended credential reuse'. TECH-H003 selected for most detailed technical analysis of the validation flow.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Reentrancy via stateful CALL in _tryValidateCredential to external role providers during hook execution
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same vulnerability: _tryValidateCredential uses call(gas(), providerAddress, ...) allowing a malicious provider to reenter the hooks or market contract between the memory read of _lenderStatus and its storage write. Note: TECH2-H003 here refers to the entry titled 'Reentrancy via _tryValidateCredential stateful call to untrusted role providers'. TECH-H004 selected for most specific description of the read-modify-write storage corruption mechanism.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Gas griefing DoS via unbounded pull provider loop with staticcall(gas(), ...) forwarding all remaining gas to external providers
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same root cause: _loopTryGetCredential iterates over _pullProviders with staticcall(gas(), providerAddress, ...) passing all remaining gas, and a single malicious/buggy provider can consume all gas causing OOG. OPS-H012 adds the getLenderStatus view angle but same root cause. Note: TECH2-H004 here refers to the entry titled 'Gas Griefing / DoS via _tryGetCredential staticcall'. OPS-H006 selected for clear and comprehensive description covering both contracts.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: HooksConfig.onBorrow uses RepayHook_Base_Size constant instead of BorrowHook_Base_Size for calldata size calculation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the identical code defect: onBorrow's assembly uses RepayHook_Base_Size (0x0224) instead of BorrowHook_Base_Size (0x0224). Currently no functional impact since values are equal, but a maintenance hazard. TECH-H006 selected for most comprehensive description.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MarketState.withdrawableProtocolFees underflow when totalAssets < normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals causes DoS on fee collection
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same arithmetic underflow: totalAssets - state.normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals reverts in Solidity 0.8.x without satSub when market is undercollateralized. Note: both entries with ID TECH2-H006 are about this same underflow and are removed. ECON2-H013 selected for explicitly contrasting with borrowableAssets which correctly uses satSub, providing strongest evidence of the defect.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: HooksConfig.onSetAnnualInterestAndReserveRatioBips checks returndatasize() before the external call due to Yul left-to-right evaluation order
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same defect: or(lt(returndatasize(), 0x40), iszero(call(...))) evaluates returndatasize() before the call opcode in Yul, checking stale return data. OPS-H002 selected for CRITICAL severity rating and more detailed explanation of the Yul evaluation semantics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: HooksConfig calldata suffix forwarding allows attacker-controlled extraData to reach hook provider selection and credential validation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same issue: HooksConfig forwards trailing calldata (calldatasize() - baseCalldataSize) as extraData to hooks, which hooks interpret as provider address and credential bytes. If markets don't sanitize trailing calldata, attackers control provider selection. Note: TECH2-H003 here refers to the entry titled 'HooksConfig calldata suffix parsing may mis-handle ABI layouts'. TECH-H011 selected for listing more affected functions and being more comprehensive.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: grantRoles unbounded loop over user-supplied arrays enables gas griefing by approved role providers
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: TECH-H013 and TECH-H014 describe the identical bug in AccessControlHooks and FixedTermLoanHooks respectively; ECON2-H018 covers both contracts. All share the same root cause: unbounded for loop in grantRoles with no maximum array size. ECON2-H018 selected for covering both contracts in a single description.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Asymmetric access control configuration (transfers allowed, withdrawals require access) traps secondary market token buyers
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same scenario: market configured with transferRequiresAccess=false but withdrawalRequiresAccess=true allows uncredentialed users to receive tokens but prevents them from withdrawing, permanently trapping funds. ECON-H012 selected for more detailed scenario analysis and impact assessment.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Blocked users with existing isKnownLenderOnMarket status bypass blockFromDeposits restrictions on transfers and withdrawals
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same bypass: onTransfer skips the isBlockedFromDeposits check if isKnownLenderOnMarket[to][msg.sender] is true, allowing blocked/sanctioned users to continue receiving tokens. OPS2-H002 selected for higher severity (HIGH) and more specific proof of signal.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: FixedTermLoanHooks withdrawal access config mismatch: withdrawalRequiresAccess derived from pre-merge flags while QueueWithdrawal hook is always enabled via required flags
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same config mismatch: withdrawalRequiresAccess is set from marketHooksConfig.useOnQueueWithdrawal() BEFORE mergeFlags(config) forces QueueWithdrawal hook enabled, creating a divergence between hook invocation and access enforcement. OPS2-H012 selected for more detailed explanation of the mergeFlags timing.
